jokes.text: I don't drink. / It's chocolate.
jokes.setup: Craig refuses the minibar saying he doesn't drink
jokes.punchline: Larry clarifies he was offering chocolate, not alcohol
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective misdirection. Economical setup/payoff with clear miscommunication.

jokes.text: After we had intercourse, we got married very quickly.
jokes.setup: Normal question about dating timeline before marriage
jokes.punchline: Larry's bluntly clinical and inappropriate terminology at a funeral
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David character moment - socially inappropriate oversharing with clinical detachment at the worst possible time.

jokes.text: And then you couldn't do it after she killed herself.
jokes.setup: Craig explaining he was about to break up with Becky when they got the call
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of the terrible timing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Larry's characteristic blunt honesty about an awkward situation.

jokes.text: If it was her mother, I'd ask you to go back on the plane and wait maybe six days, a week. For an aunt? For an aunt who doesn't even live where you live...
jokes.setup: Craig asking about appropriate timing for breakup after death
jokes.punchline: Larry's calculated hierarchy of grief based on family relationship proximity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - quantifying grief based on geographic and familial distance. Classic Curb social mathematics.

jokes.text: I've never actually used it myself, but I think it works.
jokes.setup: Larry giving Craig relationship advice about saying 'you're not happy'
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's never used his own advice
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about giving untested advice, typical Larry contradiction.

jokes.text: I've got a breath problem? What-- No, I thought I saw a bug, that's creepy.
jokes.setup: Larry suddenly reacting to something, Craig assumes it's about his breath
jokes.punchline: Larry clarifying it was just a bug, not personal hygiene commentary
jokes.explanation: Minor awkward moment, functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: I don't even like to drive. And I don't like walking. So, occasionally, I don't really know what mode of transportation to....
jokes.setup: Discussing dislike of flying
jokes.punchline: Escalating to disliking all forms of transportation, creating logical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that creates a funny logical trap - if you hate all transportation, how do you move?

jokes.text: It's not gonna be funny, is it? / Mom, please. / That's what he does.
jokes.setup: Larry offering to write the obituary
jokes.punchline: Family member worried Larry will make it comedic because he's a comedy writer
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Larry's profession creating inappropriate expectations. Sets up the obituary disaster beautifully.

jokes.text: Once you've got the outfit on, you might as well wear it to its completion. / Maintain the outfit. / 'To its completion.'
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning why Larry is still wearing funeral tie
jokes.punchline: Larry's oddly formal reasoning about 'completing' an outfit, Jeff's incredulous repetition
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pseudo-logic about clothing protocol. The formal language 'to its completion' makes mundane outfit choices sound profound.

jokes.text: Killed herself. / No, she didn't. / Killed herself-- / Why? / Nobody knows, she didn't leave a note. / That is so rude, isn't that? / That's really rude.
jokes.setup: Casual discussion of how the aunt died
jokes.punchline: Treating suicide as a breach of etiquette for not leaving an explanation
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant reframing of suicide through Larry's social etiquette obsessions. Treating ultimate tragedy as mere rudeness is peak Larry.

jokes.text: If you leave your house for 10 minutes to get a container of milk you tell somebody where you went. I let my wife know before I go anywhere. Would it have killed her to leave a note?
jokes.setup: Continuing the 'rudeness' of suicide theme
jokes.punchline: The darkly ironic 'would it have killed her' about someone who already killed herself
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy - the accidental irony of 'would it have killed her' about suicide note etiquette is genuinely shocking and hilarious.

jokes.text: He broke up with her last night and.... Isn't that awful, Larry? -What kind of timing.... It's unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Cheryl tells Larry about Craig breaking up with Becky
jokes.punchline: Larry's fake outrage about the timing when he advised it
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony as Larry feigns shock at advice he gave. Sets up his later exposure.

jokes.text: If your aunt died, I would be right by your side to support you. / If my aunt killed herself, I'd encourage you to leave the house.
jokes.setup: Cheryl saying Larry should stay to support family during grief
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate contradiction based on suicide vs. natural death distinction
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing Larry's selective empathy and unusual grief hierarchy logic.

jokes.text: Can't go on. Must go on.
jokes.setup: Family discussing how they must continue after Louise's death
jokes.punchline: Larry's Beckett reference delivered deadpan in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Subtle intellectual humor - quoting Beckett's existential despair at a family funeral gathering. Highbrow reference in lowbrow context.

jokes.text: I once performed for Norman Cousins in his hospital room when he was on his deathbed. He requested me to do five minutes. And I bombed, actually, and he got much worse and he had to watch some Marx Brothers movies to actually resuscitate him
jokes.setup: Uncle talking about laughter as healing, mentions performing for Norman Cousins
jokes.punchline: His comedy was so bad it made the patient worse, requiring Marx Brothers as antidote
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to Norman Cousins' famous laughter therapy, with self-deprecating twist about bombing so badly it nearly killed him.

jokes.text: I have unlimited mileage, I can drive anywhere. / He knows what he's doing. It's right around the corner, he'll find it. I'm sure he will. Gives him pleasure to use the rental car anyway.
jokes.setup: Craig obsessing over his unlimited mileage rental car benefits
jokes.punchline: Family's patronizing attitude toward his simple pleasure in the rental car
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Craig's fixation on rental car benefits, family treating him like a child with simple pleasures.

jokes.text: How many can I get in my mouth at a time? / Six. / You say six? / Five. Three, four.
jokes.setup: Uncle attempting to fit multiple items (presumably nuts or candy) in his mouth
jokes.punchline: Family members betting on/counting his capacity like a parlor trick
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag, family entertainment during awkward funeral gathering. Basic physical comedy.

jokes.text: 'Look what they did to my Santino. Gladys, George, look what they did.'
jokes.setup: Uncle doing impressions or references during funeral gathering
jokes.punchline: Godfather reference at inappropriate family gathering
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference humor, mildly inappropriate timing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: He just kept repeating something about not being happy. We could've worked through that.
jokes.setup: Becky explaining Craig's breakup reasoning
jokes.punchline: Her dismissal of fundamental incompatibility as a minor issue to 'work through'
jokes.explanation: Callback to Larry's relationship advice, showing how his 'foolproof' line was interpreted. Character-driven irony.

jokes.text: No, we just, you know... he talked about Aunt Louise and the minibar.
jokes.setup: Becky asking if Craig said anything to Larry about the relationship
jokes.punchline: Larry's massive understatement, omitting the breakup counseling session
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry deflection - technically true but hilariously incomplete. Great callback structure building tension.

jokes.text: You need a scissor to cut the paper, right? / To cut the paper, yeah. / And scotch tape, too, right? / Right. / Do they sell all that in the same store? / No, I don't believe they do.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to figure out gift-wrapping logistics
jokes.punchline: The absurd complexity of acquiring basic wrapping supplies across multiple stores
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry interaction - turning simple task into complex logistical nightmare through overthinking.

jokes.text: How many stores are we talking about?
jokes.setup: Continuing gift-wrap supply chain investigation
jokes.punchline: Larry treating gift-wrapping like a major logistical operation
jokes.explanation: Escalation of gift-wrap obsession. Larry's delivery makes mundane question sound like military planning.

jokes.text: 'Devoted sister, beloved cunt'?
jokes.setup: Family discovering the obituary in the newspaper
jokes.punchline: The horrific typo turning 'aunt' into profanity
jokes.explanation: Legendary comedic moment - the setup pays off with shocking profanity in newspaper obituary. Perfect structure and maximum impact.

jokes.text: The woman leads a decent life and this is how she ends up? 'Beloved cunt'?
jokes.setup: Continuing reaction to obituary typo
jokes.punchline: The existential injustice of life's final indignity
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the typo revelation, treating it as cosmic injustice rather than simple mistake.

jokes.text: I'm just glad you weren't in charge of the headstone!
jokes.setup: Family's horror at obituary mistake
jokes.punchline: Imagining Larry making the same mistake on permanent memorial
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation - if Larry botched newspaper, imagine permanent stone carving. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: 'It wasn't her mother who died, it was just her aunt. You should break up with her today. You don't look happy, you should break up!'
jokes.setup: Becky confronting Larry about Craig's exact words
jokes.punchline: Revealing Larry's calculated advice verbatim, showing his callous calculation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure - Larry's earlier advice comes back to destroy him. Shows his social calculating exposed.

jokes.text: I told him to wait till he got back to Tallahassee! I didn't say to do it last night at the wake.
jokes.setup: Larry being confronted about the breakup advice
jokes.punchline: His defense focuses on timing technicality rather than the advice itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - missing the moral point entirely, focusing on technical details of his advice.

jokes.text: He's a bad guy, I was doing you a favor!
jokes.setup: Larry defending his interference in Becky's relationship
jokes.punchline: Claiming altruistic motives for his meddling disaster
jokes.explanation: Larry's delusional self-justification - turning his social destruction into heroic intervention.

jokes.text: I don't give a shit.
jokes.setup: Store clerk asking what kind of wrapping paper Larry wants
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete breakdown over gift-wrapping complexity
jokes.explanation: Larry's exasperation reaching breaking point. Simple profanity but perfectly timed character moment.

jokes.text: What kind of present is that? Not wrapped nicely. / Yeah, I'm sorry about that. I looked in stores, I couldn't find any wrapping store. There's no wrapping stores anywhere?
jokes.setup: Jeff's mother criticizing the unwrapped gift presentation
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive explanation and incredulous question about wrapping stores
jokes.explanation: Callback to wrapping paper saga paying off. Larry's genuine bewilderment about 'wrapping stores' shows his disconnect from normal life.

jokes.text: Elizabeth Taylor wears glasses. She has the exact same glasses! / I can't use 'em. I have a prescription in my lenses.
jokes.setup: Larry proudly presenting Elizabeth Taylor sunglasses as perfect gift
jokes.punchline: Immediate rejection due to practical prescription lens needs
jokes.explanation: Classic gift-giving failure - Larry's excitement met with practical reality. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: 'Louise Hoenin, devoted sister, beloved cunt'? / What? It says that in the paper? Let me see this. / That's unbelievable! / I know! You said 'aunt,' I wrote down 'aunt'! That's bullshit!
jokes.setup: Jeff discovering the obituary typo for the first time
jokes.punchline: Jeff's shock and Larry's defensive reaction
jokes.explanation: Extending the obituary disaster to new characters. Jeff's fresh shock gives the joke new life.

jokes.text: What are they gonna say? 'We're sorry we called her a cunt, we meant aunt'?
jokes.setup: Discussion of newspaper printing retraction for obituary
jokes.punchline: The impossibly awkward wording any retraction would require
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of the obituary joke - imagining the equally impossible retraction. Great observational humor about bureaucratic absurdity.

jokes.text: Do me a favor. When I die, let someone else handle the obit, okay?
jokes.setup: After witnessing Larry's obituary disaster
jokes.punchline: Jeff's deadpan request for future obituary planning
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback capper - Jeff learning from Larry's mistake and preemptively protecting his own legacy. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: You copped a feel off my mom, you gotta go.
jokes.setup: Larry staying over at Jeff's
jokes.punchline: Accusation of inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Good shock revelation that creates new disaster from innocent moment.

jokes.text: She said, 'Larry touched my bosom for several seconds.'
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his mother's complaint about Larry
jokes.punchline: The formal, old-fashioned language 'bosom' and precise timing measurement
jokes.explanation: Funny language choice - 'bosom' and exact timing make innocent accident sound calculated and creepy.

jokes.text: She was looking in the toaster and I was holding the toaster... she wanted to get a better look at her face in it and she leaned against the back of my hand... and so, I didn't want to just turn.... I didn't want to hurt her feelings by taking it away fast.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the innocent circumstances of the 'groping'
jokes.punchline: His overly detailed, defensive explanation making him sound more guilty
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry over-explanation that makes innocent situation sound suspicious. Perfect character moment of good intentions gone wrong.

jokes.text: She rubbed up against the back of my hand! I didn't do anything!
jokes.setup: Larry defending himself against groping accusation
jokes.punchline: His specific explanation that makes it sound worse
jokes.explanation: Larry's protest makes him sound guiltier. Good cringe development.

jokes.text: I'll dream about fucking your mother. 'Sweet dreams.'
jokes.setup: Jeff's sarcastic 'sweet dreams' dismissal
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocking, inappropriate response escalating the conflict
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation showing Larry's worst impulses when cornered. Dark but character-consistent response.

jokes.text: Even when I was dating, I would wait four months before I would try and make a breast move, you know? / I understand. / Not in their kitchen, in front of their daughter-in-law and son--
jokes.setup: Larry defending his character regarding inappropriate touching
jokes.punchline: His detailed explanation of his 'breast move' timeline and inappropriate venue awareness
jokes.explanation: Larry's misguided attempt to establish his moral standards ends up sounding creepier. Classic Larry self-destruction.

jokes.text: That's called 'Indian giving.' / I know what it's called. / It's a very racist term, but I'm okay with that.
jokes.setup: Jeff pointing out Larry taking back the gift is 'Indian giving'
jokes.punchline: Larry acknowledging the racism but prioritizing his petty revenge
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Larry's moral flexibility when it serves his interests. Self-aware about racism but prioritizes petty revenge.

jokes.text: Wake him up, get him out, that's my room.
jokes.setup: Larry at hotel where he's paying for Craig's room
jokes.punchline: Demanding Craig be evicted from the room Larry is paying for
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's petty revenge. Logical but vindictive response.

jokes.text: The room's paid for through tomorrow. The cards have been run for this evening. He's authorized to be there till 11:00 a.m. tomorrow, check-out time. Our manager will work out the bill with you, if you come back tomorrow at 10:00.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to kick Craig out of hotel room he's paying for
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic policies preventing Larry from exercising what he thinks is his right
jokes.explanation: Bureaucratic frustration comedy - Larry's revenge thwarted by hotel policies. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm Frank Sinatra, Jr., you're turning me away tonight? Jr. walks in and you turn him away? The Pope is being shooed out into the street. Are you going to shoo The Pope out?
jokes.setup: Hotel has no rooms available for Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalating hypothetical celebrity rejections, ending with The Pope
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from minor celebrity to religious leader. Larry's indignation at hypothetical celebrity treatment is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Elizabeth Taylor sunglasses. / They're very nice-- / Elizabeth Taylor wears these sunglasses. / Thank you, sir, I wear prescription. Thank you very much.
jokes.setup: Larry desperately trying to bribe hotel clerk with sunglasses
jokes.punchline: Another prescription lens rejection of his 'perfect' gift
jokes.explanation: Third rejection of the sunglasses, establishing them as Larry's cursed gift. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: Thanks for ratting me out to Becky, really appreciated it. / I did not rat you out. / You told her everything we talked about. And I told you not to say anything. / That's your problem.
jokes.setup: Craig confronting Larry about exposing his breakup consultation
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive 'that's your problem' response to legitimate complaint
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier plot thread with Larry showing zero remorse. Characteristic deflection of responsibility.

jokes.text: Can I interest you in some sunglasses?
jokes.setup: Larry being escorted out of hotel lobby
jokes.punchline: Final desperate attempt to peddle the cursed sunglasses
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending to the sunglasses saga - Larry's last-ditch sales attempt while being ejected. Great callback closure.
